- newString:

```
            
            // Load descriptions from JSON file
            Debug.Log("[StationpediaPlus] Loading descriptions...");
            LoadDescriptions();
            
            // Apply Harmony patches
            Debug.Log("[StationpediaPlus] Applying Harmony patches...");
            ApplyHarmonyPatches();
            
            // On hot-reload, skip Regenerate() - it fails because Stationpedia's internal state
            // has references to destroyed Unity objects. Pages will update when viewed.
            // We detect hot-reload by checking if Stationpedia.Instance exists (it wouldn't during cold start).
            Log?.LogInfo($"Stationpedia.Instance = {Stationpedia.Instance}");
            Debug.Log($"[StationpediaPlus] Stationpedia.Instance = {Stationpedia.Instance}");
            if (Stationpedia.Instance != null)
            {
                // Don't call Regenerate() - it causes NullReferenceException during hot-reload
                // because the Stationpedia's CreatedCategories list has destroyed object references.
                // Instead, just close and re-open the Stationpedia to refresh, or navigate to a new page.
                Log?.LogInfo(">>> Skipping Regenerate() to avoid hot-reload crash <<<");
                Debug.Log("[StationpediaPlus] Skipping Regenerate() - pages will refresh when navigated");
                ConsoleWindow.Print("[StationpediaPlus] Patches applied! Navigate to a page to see changes.");
            }
            else
            {
                Log?.LogWarning("Stationpedia.Instance is NULL - cannot regenerate yet");
                Debug.Log("[StationpediaPlus] Stationpedia.Instance is null - patches will apply later");
                ConsoleWindow.Print("[StationpediaPlus] Stationpedia not yet initialized - patches will apply on first open");
            }
            
            // Start the monitoring coroutine
            Debug.Log("[StationpediaPlus] Starting monitoring coroutine...");
            _monitorCoroutine = StartCoroutine(MonitorStationpediaCoroutine());
            ConsoleWindow.Print("[StationpediaPlus] Started monitoring coroutine");
            
            _initialized = true;
            Debug.Log("[StationpediaPlus] Initialize COMPLETE!");
            ConsoleWindow.Print("[StationpediaPlus] Initialized successfully!");
```

- oldString:

```
            
            // Load descriptions from JSON file
            Debug.Log("[StationpediaPlus] Loading descriptions...");
            LoadDescriptions();
            
            // Apply Harmony patches
            Debug.Log("[StationpediaPlus] Applying Harmony patches...");
            ApplyHarmonyPatches();
            
            // Regenerate Stationpedia to apply our patches
            // This is critical for hot-reload via ScriptEngine - by the time our mod loads,
            // Stationpedia has already been built. We need to rebuild it with our patches.
            Log?.LogInfo($"Stationpedia.Instance = {Stationpedia.Instance}");
            Debug.Log($"[StationpediaPlus] Stationpedia.Instance = {Stationpedia.Instance}");
            if (Stationpedia.Instance != null)
            {
                Log?.LogInfo(">>> REGENERATING STATIONPEDIA NOW <<<");
                Debug.Log("[StationpediaPlus] Regenerating Stationpedia...");
                ConsoleWindow.Print("[StationpediaPlus] Regenerating Stationpedia to apply patches...");
                
                try
                {
                    Stationpedia.Regenerate();
                    Log?.LogInfo(">>> STATIONPEDIA REGENERATED SUCCESSFULLY <<<");
                    Debug.Log("[StationpediaPlus] Stationpedia regenerated!");
                    ConsoleWindow.Print("[StationpediaPlus] Stationpedia regenerated!");
                }
                catch (Exception ex)
                {
                    Log?.LogError($"Failed to regenerate Stationpedia: {ex}");
                    Debug.LogError($"[StationpediaPlus] Failed to regenerate: {ex}");
                }
            }
            else
            {
                Log?.LogWarning("Stationpedia.Instance is NULL - cannot regenerate yet");
                Debug.Log("[StationpediaPlus] Stationpedia.Instance is null - patches will apply later");
                ConsoleWindow.Print("[StationpediaPlus] Stationpedia not yet initialized - patches will apply on first open");
            }
            
            // Start the monitoring coroutine
            Debug.Log("[StationpediaPlus] Starting monitoring coroutine...");
            _monitorCoroutine = StartCoroutine(MonitorStationpediaCoroutine());
            ConsoleWindow.Print("[StationpediaPlus] Started monitoring coroutine");
            
            _initialized = true;
            Debug.Log("[StationpediaPlus] Initialize COMPLETE!");
            ConsoleWindow.Print("[StationpediaPlus] Initialized successfully!");
```


</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

The error is happening in the original `ChangeDisplay` method before our postfix even runs. The stack trace shows:

```
at (wrapper dynamic-method) assets.scripts.ui.universalpage.dmd<assets.scripts.ui.universalpage::changedisplay>
```

This is Harmony's dynamically modified version of the original method. The error is at `unityengine.component.get_gameobject` - meaning something in the original `ChangeDisplay` method is trying to access `.gameObject` on a destroyed component.

The issue is that when we do `Stationpedia.Regenerate()`, the original ChangeDisplay method tries to access UI elements that were destroyed during our cleanup. 

The problem is NOT in our postfix - it's that our cleanup is destroying objects that the original ChangeDisplay method expects to exist.

Let me check how the cleanup is happening and what we're destroying. The issue might be that when we unpatch Harmony, then re-patch and call Regenerate(), the Stationpedia's internal state has references to destroyed objects.

Actually, looking more carefully:
1. OnDestroy cleans up components and GameObjects
2. Then Awake() runs and patches again
3. Then we call Regenerate() which calls ChangeDisplay
4. ChangeDisplay (the original method) tries to use something that was destroyed

The problem might be that we need to delay the regeneration, or we need to not destroy certain things.

Wait - looking at the error more carefully, it says "Cleaning up 0 created GameObjects" in many cases. So we're not destroying our GameObjects. The issue is something else.

Let me think about this differently. The error happens during regeneration which is called from Initialize at line 337. The original ChangeDisplay method is failing because it's trying to access a gameObject of a destroyed component.

This could be the Stationpedia's own internal state - things like `CreatedCategories` that hold references to destroyed objects from the previous render.


I'll investigate skipping the regeneration in Initialize. Avoiding immediate regeneration might prevent the component access issues we're seeing. By deferring the regeneration, we could allow the UI to stabilize before attempting to rebuild the Stationpedia content.

```
                // Unity-safe null checks - destroyed objects are "fake null"
                if ((object)__instance == null || !__instance) return;
                if ((object)page == null) return;
                
                // Check if the Content transform is valid (not destroyed)
                UnityEngine.Transform contentTransform;
                try
                {
                    contentTransform = __instance.Content;
                    if ((object)contentTransform == null || !contentTransform) return;
                    // Try to access gameObject to verify it's not destroyed
                    var testGO = contentTransform.gameObject;
                    if ((object)testGO == null || !testGO) return;
                }
                catch
                {
                    return; // Content is destroyed
                }
                
                string pageKey = page.Key;
                if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(pageKey)) return;

                if (!StationpediaPlusMod.DeviceDatabase.TryGetValue(pageKey, out var deviceDesc)) return;
                if (deviceDesc.specialBehaviors == null || deviceDesc.specialBehaviors.Count == 0) return;

                var stationpedia = Stationpedia.Instance;
                if ((object)stationpedia == null || !stationpedia) return;
                
                var categoryPrefab = stationpedia.CategoryPrefab;
                var genericPrefab = stationpedia.GenericPrefab;
                
                if ((object)categoryPrefab == null || !categoryPrefab) return;
                if ((object)genericPrefab == null || !genericPrefab) return;

                // Create the collapsible category container (like "Logic" or "Internal Memory")
                StationpediaCategory category = UnityEngine.Object.Instantiate<StationpediaCategory>(
                    categoryPrefab, contentTransform);
                if ((object)category == null || !category) return;
                if ((object)category.Title == null || !category.Title) return;
                if ((object)category.Contents == null || !category.Contents) return;
                
                category.Title.text = "Special Behaviors";

                // Create ONE SPDAGeneric item containing ALL behaviors
                // This puts everything in a single box like the game does for descriptions
                SPDAGeneric item = UnityEngine.Object.Instantiate<SPDAGeneric>(
                    genericPrefab, category.Contents);
                
                if ((object)item == null || !item) return;
                
                // Build combined text for all behaviors
                var sb = new System.Text.StringBuilder();
                bool first = true;
                foreach (var behavior in deviceDesc.specialBehaviors)
                {
                    if (!first) sb.AppendLine().AppendLine(); // Double line break between behaviors
                    sb.AppendLine($"<color=#FFD700><b>{behavior.title}</b></color>");
                    sb.Append(behavior.description);
                    first = false;
                }
                
                // Hide the header - we'll put everything in Info
                if ((object)item.Header != null && item.Header)
                {
                    item.Header.gameObject.SetActive(false);
                }
                
                // Set the info (all behaviors combined)
                if ((object)item.Info != null && item.Info)
                {
                    item.Info.text = sb.ToString();
                    item.Info.enableWordWrapping = true;
                    item.Info.overflowMode = TMPro.TextOverflowModes.Overflow;
                    item.Info.richText = true;
                }
                
                // Hide the icon if present
                if ((object)item.Icon != null && item.Icon)
                {
                    item.Icon.gameObject.SetActive(false);
                }

                // Make sure contents are visible
                category.Contents.gameObject.SetActive(true);
                
                // Add to game's category list so it handles cleanup
                __instance.CreatedCategories.Add(category);
            }
            catch (Exception)
            {
            }
        }
```

```
        public static void ChangeDisplay_Postfix(Assets.Scripts.UI.UniversalPage __instance, StationpediaPage page)
        {
            try
            {
                // Unity-safe null checks - destroyed objects are "fake null"
                if ((object)__instance == null || !__instance) return;
                if ((object)page == null) return;
                
                // Check if the Content transform is valid (not destroyed)
                UnityEngine.Transform contentTransform;
                try
                {
                    contentTransform = __instance.Content;
                    if ((object)contentTransform == null || !contentTransform) return;
                    // Try to access gameObject to verify it's not destroyed
                    var testGO = contentTransform.gameObject;
                    if ((object)testGO == null || !testGO) return;
                }
                catch
                {
                    return; // Content is destroyed
                }
                
                string pageKey = page.Key;
                if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(pageKey)) return;

                if (!StationpediaPlusMod.DeviceDatabase.TryGetValue(pageKey, out var deviceDesc)) return;
                if (deviceDesc.specialBehaviors == null || deviceDesc.specialBehaviors.Count == 0) return;

                var stationpedia = Stationpedia.Instance;
                if ((object)stationpedia == null || !stationpedia) return;
                
                var categoryPrefab = stationpedia.CategoryPrefab;
                var genericPrefab = stationpedia.GenericPrefab;
                
                if ((object)categoryPrefab == null || !categoryPrefab) return;
                if ((object)genericPrefab == null || !genericPrefab) return;

                // Create the collapsible category container (like "Logic" or "Internal Memory")
                StationpediaCategory category = UnityEngine.Object.Instantiate<StationpediaCategory>(
                    categoryPrefab, contentTransform);
                if ((object)category == null || !category) return;
                if ((object)category.Title == null || !category.Title) return;
                if ((object)category.Contents == null || !category.Contents) return;
                
                category.Title.text = "Special Behaviors";

                // Add each behavior as a SPDAGeneric item (like Internal Memory instructions)
                // This uses the game's built-in prefab which auto-sizes properly
                foreach (var behavior in deviceDesc.specialBehaviors)
                {
                    SPDAGeneric item = UnityEngine.Object.Instantiate<SPDAGeneric>(
                        genericPrefab, category.Contents);
                    
                    if ((object)item == null || !item) continue;
                    
                    // Set the header (title) with gold color
                    if ((object)item.Header != null && item.Header)
                    {
                        item.Header.text = $"<color=#FFD700>{behavior.title}</color>";
                    }
                    
                    // Set the info (description) - this is the main text area
                    if ((object)item.Info != null && item.Info)
                    {
                        item.Info.text = behavior.description;
                        // Enable word wrap for long descriptions
                        item.Info.enableWordWrapping = true;
                        item.Info.overflowMode = TMPro.TextOverflowModes.Overflow;
                    }
                    
                    // Hide the icon if present (we don't need it for text-only items)
                    if ((object)item.Icon != null && item.Icon)
                    {
                        item.Icon.gameObject.SetActive(false);
                    }
                }

                // Make sure contents are visible
                category.Contents.gameObject.SetActive(true);
                
                // Add to game's category list so it handles cleanup
                __instance.CreatedCategories.Add(category);
            }
            catch (Exception)
            {
            }
        }
```


</details></tool-use>

```
        public static void ChangeDisplay_Postfix(Assets.Scripts.UI.UniversalPage __instance, StationpediaPage page)
        {
            try
            {
                // Unity-safe null checks - destroyed objects are "fake null"
                if ((object)__instance == null || !__instance) return;
                if ((object)page == null) return;
                
                // Check if the Content transform is valid (not destroyed)
                UnityEngine.Transform contentTransform;
                try
                {
                    contentTransform = __instance.Content;
                    if ((object)contentTransform == null || !contentTransform) return;
                    // Try to access gameObject to verify it's not destroyed
                    var testGO = contentTransform.gameObject;
                    if ((object)testGO == null || !testGO) return;
                }
                catch
                {
                    return; // Content is destroyed
                }
                
                string pageKey = page.Key;
                if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(pageKey)) return;

                if (!StationpediaPlusMod.DeviceDatabase.TryGetValue(pageKey, out var deviceDesc)) return;
                if (deviceDesc.specialBehaviors == null || deviceDesc.specialBehaviors.Count == 0) return;

                var stationpedia = Stationpedia.Instance;
                if ((object)stationpedia == null || !stationpedia) return;
                
                var categoryPrefab = stationpedia.CategoryPrefab;
                var genericPrefab = stationpedia.GenericPrefab;
                
                if ((object)categoryPrefab == null || !categoryPrefab) return;
                if ((object)genericPrefab == null || !genericPrefab) return;

                // Create the collapsible category container (like "Logic" or "Internal Memory")
                StationpediaCategory category = UnityEngine.Object.Instantiate<StationpediaCategory>(
                    categoryPrefab, contentTransform);
                if ((object)category == null || !category) return;
                if ((object)category.Title == null || !category.Title) return;
                if ((object)category.Contents == null || !category.Contents) return;
                
                category.Title.text = "Special Behaviors";

                // Create ONE SPDAGeneric item containing ALL behaviors
                SPDAGeneric item = UnityEngine.Object.Instantiate<SPDAGeneric>(
                    genericPrefab, category.Contents);
                
                if ((object)item == null || !item) return;
                
                // Build combined text for all behaviors
                var sb = new System.Text.StringBuilder();
                bool first = true;
                foreach (var behavior in deviceDesc.specialBehaviors)
                {
                    if (!first) sb.AppendLine().AppendLine(); // Double line break between behaviors
                    sb.AppendLine($"<color=#FFD700><b>{behavior.title}</b></color>");
                    sb.Append(behavior.description);
                    first = false;
                }
                
                // Hide the header - we'll put everything in Info
                if ((object)item.Header != null && item.Header)
                {
                    item.Header.gameObject.SetActive(false);
                }
                
                // Set the info (all behaviors combined)
                if ((object)item.Info != null && item.Info)
                {
                    item.Info.text = sb.ToString();
                    item.Info.enableWordWrapping = true;
                    item.Info.overflowMode = TMPro.TextOverflowModes.Overflow;
                    item.Info.richText = true;
                    
                    // Force text mesh to update so we can get preferred height
                    item.Info.ForceMeshUpdate();
                    
                    // Get the preferred height of the text
                    float textHeight = item.Info.preferredHeight;
                    
                    // Set the item's RectTransform to fit the text with some padding
                    var itemRect = item.GetComponent<UnityEngine.RectTransform>();
                    if (itemRect != null)
                    {
                        // Set the height to fit the text plus padding
                        itemRect.SetSizeWithCurrentAnchors(UnityEngine.RectTransform.Axis.Vertical, textHeight + 20f);
                    }
                    
                    // Also resize the Info's RectTransform
                    var infoRect = item.Info.GetComponent<UnityEngine.RectTransform>();
                    if (infoRect != null)
                    {
                        infoRect.SetSizeWithCurrentAnchors(UnityEngine.RectTransform.Axis.Vertical, textHeight);
                    }
                }
                
                // Hide the icon if present
                if ((object)item.Icon != null && item.Icon)
                {
                    item.Icon.gameObject.SetActive(false);
                }

                // Make sure contents are visible
                category.Contents.gameObject.SetActive(true);
                
                // Add to game's category list so it handles cleanup
                __instance.CreatedCategories.Add(category);
            }
            catch (Exception)
            {
            }
        }
```

```
        public static void ChangeDisplay_Postfix(Assets.Scripts.UI.UniversalPage __instance, StationpediaPage page)
        {
            try
            {
                // Unity-safe null checks - destroyed objects are "fake null"
                if ((object)__instance == null || !__instance) return;
                if ((object)page == null) return;
                
                // Check if the Content transform is valid (not destroyed)
                UnityEngine.Transform contentTransform;
                try
                {
                    contentTransform = __instance.Content;
                    if ((object)contentTransform == null || !contentTransform) return;
                    // Try to access gameObject to verify it's not destroyed
                    var testGO = contentTransform.gameObject;
                    if ((object)testGO == null || !testGO) return;
                }
                catch
                {
                    return; // Content is destroyed
                }
                
                string pageKey = page.Key;
                if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(pageKey)) return;

                if (!StationpediaPlusMod.DeviceDatabase.TryGetValue(pageKey, out var deviceDesc)) return;
                if (deviceDesc.specialBehaviors == null || deviceDesc.specialBehaviors.Count == 0) return;

                var stationpedia = Stationpedia.Instance;
                if ((object)stationpedia == null || !stationpedia) return;
                
                var categoryPrefab = stationpedia.CategoryPrefab;
                var genericPrefab = stationpedia.GenericPrefab;
                
                if ((object)categoryPrefab == null || !categoryPrefab) return;
                if ((object)genericPrefab == null || !genericPrefab) return;

                // Create the collapsible category container (like "Logic" or "Internal Memory")
                StationpediaCategory category = UnityEngine.Object.Instantiate<StationpediaCategory>(
                    categoryPrefab, contentTransform);
                if ((object)category == null || !category) return;
                if ((object)category.Title == null || !category.Title) return;
                if ((object)category.Contents == null || !category.Contents) return;
                
                category.Title.text = "Special Behaviors";

                // Create ONE SPDAGeneric item containing ALL behaviors
                // This puts everything in a single box like the game does for descriptions
                SPDAGeneric item = UnityEngine.Object.Instantiate<SPDAGeneric>(
                    genericPrefab, category.Contents);
                
                if ((object)item == null || !item) return;
                
                // Build combined text for all behaviors
                var sb = new System.Text.StringBuilder();
                bool first = true;
                foreach (var behavior in deviceDesc.specialBehaviors)
                {
                    if (!first) sb.AppendLine().AppendLine(); // Double line break between behaviors
                    sb.AppendLine($"<color=#FFD700><b>{behavior.title}</b></color>");
                    sb.Append(behavior.description);
                    first = false;
                }
                
                // Hide the header - we'll put everything in Info
                if ((object)item.Header != null && item.Header)
                {
                    item.Header.gameObject.SetActive(false);
                }
                
                // Set the info (all behaviors combined)
                if ((object)item.Info != null && item.Info)
                {
                    item.Info.text = sb.ToString();
                    item.Info.enableWordWrapping = true;
                    item.Info.overflowMode = TMPro.TextOverflowModes.Overflow;
                    item.Info.richText = true;
                    
                    // Force the text to auto-size vertically
                    var infoRect = item.Info.GetComponent<UnityEngine.RectTransform>();
                    if (infoRect != null)
                    {
                        // Make width stretch to parent, height auto-size
                        infoRect.anchorMin = new UnityEngine.Vector2(0, 1);
                        infoRect.anchorMax = new UnityEngine.Vector2(1, 1);
                        infoRect.pivot = new UnityEngine.Vector2(0.5f, 1);
                    }
                    
                    // Add ContentSizeFitter to Info text to auto-expand height
                    var infoFitter = item.Info.gameObject.GetComponent<UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter>();
                    if (infoFitter == null)
                    {
                        infoFitter = item.Info.gameObject.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter>();
                    }
                    infoFitter.horizontalFit = UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.Unconstrained;
                    infoFitter.verticalFit = UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.PreferredSize;
                }
                
                // Add ContentSizeFitter to the item itself to expand based on content
                var itemFitter = item.gameObject.GetComponent<UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter>();
                if (itemFitter == null)
                {
                    itemFitter = item.gameObject.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter>();
                }
                itemFitter.horizontalFit = UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.Unconstrained;
                itemFitter.verticalFit = UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.PreferredSize;
                
                // Add LayoutElement with flexible width
                var itemLayout = item.gameObject.GetComponent<UnityEngine.UI.LayoutElement>();
                if (itemLayout == null)
                {
                    itemLayout = item.gameObject.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.LayoutElement>();
                }
                itemLayout.flexibleWidth = 1;
                
                // Hide the icon if present
                if ((object)item.Icon != null && item.Icon)
                {
                    item.Icon.gameObject.SetActive(false);
                }

                // Make sure contents are visible
                category.Contents.gameObject.SetActive(true);
                
                // Force layout rebuild
                UnityEngine.UI.LayoutRebuilder.ForceRebuildLayoutImmediate(item.GetComponent<UnityEngine.RectTransform>());
                UnityEngine.UI.LayoutRebuilder.ForceRebuildLayoutImmediate(category.Contents);
                
                // Add to game's category list so it handles cleanup
                __instance.CreatedCategories.Add(category);
            }
            catch (Exception)
            {
            }
        }
```

```
                var stationpedia = Stationpedia.Instance;
                if ((object)stationpedia == null || !stationpedia) return;
                
                var categoryPrefab = stationpedia.CategoryPrefab;
                if ((object)categoryPrefab == null || !categoryPrefab) return;

                // Create the collapsible category container (like "Logic" or "Internal Memory")
                StationpediaCategory category = UnityEngine.Object.Instantiate<StationpediaCategory>(
                    categoryPrefab, contentTransform);
                if ((object)category == null || !category) return;
                if ((object)category.Title == null || !category.Title) return;
                if ((object)category.Contents == null || !category.Contents) return;
                
                category.Title.text = "Special Behaviors";

                // Build combined text for all behaviors
                var sb = new System.Text.StringBuilder();
                bool first = true;
                foreach (var behavior in deviceDesc.specialBehaviors)
                {
                    if (!first) sb.AppendLine().AppendLine(); // Double line break between behaviors
                    sb.AppendLine($"<color=#FFD700><b>{behavior.title}</b></color>");
                    sb.Append(behavior.description);
                    first = false;
                }
                
                // Instead of using SPDAGeneric prefab (which has fixed height), create a raw TMP text element
                // This lets the layout system size it properly based on content
                var textGO = new UnityEngine.GameObject("SpecialBehaviorsText");
                textGO.transform.SetParent(category.Contents, false);
                
                // Add RectTransform (required for UI)
                var rectTransform = textGO.AddComponent<UnityEngine.RectTransform>();
                
                // Set anchors to stretch horizontally but fit vertically
                rectTransform.anchorMin = new UnityEngine.Vector2(0, 1);
                rectTransform.anchorMax = new UnityEngine.Vector2(1, 1);
                rectTransform.pivot = new UnityEngine.Vector2(0.5f, 1);
                
                // Add the text component
                var textComponent = textGO.AddComponent<TMPro.TextMeshProUGUI>();
                textComponent.text = sb.ToString();
                textComponent.enableWordWrapping = true;
                textComponent.overflowMode = TMPro.TextOverflowModes.Overflow;
                textComponent.richText = true;
                textComponent.fontSize = 16;
                textComponent.color = UnityEngine.Color.white;
                textComponent.margin = new UnityEngine.Vector4(10, 5, 10, 5); // left, top, right, bottom padding
                
                // Add LayoutElement so the vertical layout group respects preferred size
                var layoutElement = textGO.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.LayoutElement>();
                layoutElement.flexibleWidth = 1;
                
                // Force text to calculate its preferred height
                textComponent.ForceMeshUpdate();
                float preferredHeight = textComponent.preferredHeight;
                
                // Set the preferred height on the layout element
                layoutElement.preferredHeight = preferredHeight + 10f; // Add some padding
                layoutElement.minHeight = preferredHeight + 10f;
                
                // Make sure contents are visible
                category.Contents.gameObject.SetActive(true);
                
                // Add to game's category list so it handles cleanup
                __instance.CreatedCategories.Add(category);
```

```
                var stationpedia = Stationpedia.Instance;
                if ((object)stationpedia == null || !stationpedia) return;
                
                var categoryPrefab = stationpedia.CategoryPrefab;
                var genericPrefab = stationpedia.GenericPrefab;
                
                if ((object)categoryPrefab == null || !categoryPrefab) return;
                if ((object)genericPrefab == null || !genericPrefab) return;

                // Create the collapsible category container (like "Logic" or "Internal Memory")
                StationpediaCategory category = UnityEngine.Object.Instantiate<StationpediaCategory>(
                    categoryPrefab, contentTransform);
                if ((object)category == null || !category) return;
                if ((object)category.Title == null || !category.Title) return;
                if ((object)category.Contents == null || !category.Contents) return;
                
                category.Title.text = "Special Behaviors";

                // Create ONE SPDAGeneric item containing ALL behaviors
                SPDAGeneric item = UnityEngine.Object.Instantiate<SPDAGeneric>(
                    genericPrefab, category.Contents);
                
                if ((object)item == null || !item) return;
                
                // Build combined text for all behaviors
                var sb = new System.Text.StringBuilder();
                bool first = true;
                foreach (var behavior in deviceDesc.specialBehaviors)
                {
                    if (!first) sb.AppendLine().AppendLine(); // Double line break between behaviors
                    sb.AppendLine($"<color=#FFD700><b>{behavior.title}</b></color>");
                    sb.Append(behavior.description);
                    first = false;
                }
                
                // Hide the header - we'll put everything in Info
                if ((object)item.Header != null && item.Header)
                {
                    item.Header.gameObject.SetActive(false);
                }
                
                // Set the info (all behaviors combined)
                if ((object)item.Info != null && item.Info)
                {
                    item.Info.text = sb.ToString();
                    item.Info.enableWordWrapping = true;
                    item.Info.overflowMode = TMPro.TextOverflowModes.Overflow;
                    item.Info.richText = true;
                    
                    // Force text mesh to update so we can get preferred height
                    item.Info.ForceMeshUpdate();
                    
                    // Get the preferred height of the text
                    float textHeight = item.Info.preferredHeight;
                    
                    // Set the item's RectTransform to fit the text with some padding
                    var itemRect = item.GetComponent<UnityEngine.RectTransform>();
                    if (itemRect != null)
                    {
                        // Set the height to fit the text plus padding
                        itemRect.SetSizeWithCurrentAnchors(UnityEngine.RectTransform.Axis.Vertical, textHeight + 20f);
                    }
                    
                    // Also resize the Info's RectTransform
                    var infoRect = item.Info.GetComponent<UnityEngine.RectTransform>();
                    if (infoRect != null)
                    {
                        infoRect.SetSizeWithCurrentAnchors(UnityEngine.RectTransform.Axis.Vertical, textHeight);
                    }
                }
                
                // Hide the icon if present
                if ((object)item.Icon != null && item.Icon)
                {
                    item.Icon.gameObject.SetActive(false);
                }

                // Make sure contents are visible
                category.Contents.gameObject.SetActive(true);
                
                // Add to game's category list so it handles cleanup
                __instance.CreatedCategories.Add(category);
```

```
                // Instead of using SPDAGeneric prefab (which has fixed height), create a raw TMP text element

                // Get parent width to calculate wrapped text height correctly
                float parentWidth = category.Contents.rect.width;
                if (parentWidth <= 0) parentWidth = 400f; // Fallback width
                
                // Set size explicitly - stretch to parent width
                rectTransform.anchorMin = new UnityEngine.Vector2(0, 1);
                rectTransform.anchorMax = new UnityEngine.Vector2(1, 1);
                rectTransform.pivot = new UnityEngine.Vector2(0.5f, 1);
                rectTransform.sizeDelta = new UnityEngine.Vector2(0, 100); // Width from anchors, temp height
                
                // Add the text component
                var textComponent = textGO.AddComponent<TMPro.TextMeshProUGUI>();
                textComponent.text = sb.ToString();
                textComponent.enableWordWrapping = true;
                textComponent.overflowMode = TMPro.TextOverflowModes.Overflow;
                textComponent.richText = true;
                textComponent.fontSize = 16;
                textComponent.color = UnityEngine.Color.white;
                textComponent.margin = new UnityEngine.Vector4(10, 5, 10, 5); // left, top, right, bottom padding
                
                // Set the rect width explicitly so TMP can calculate wrapped height
                float textWidth = parentWidth - 20f; // Account for margins
                textComponent.rectTransform.SetSizeWithCurrentAnchors(UnityEngine.RectTransform.Axis.Horizontal, textWidth);
                
                // Add LayoutElement so the vertical layout group respects preferred size
                var layoutElement = textGO.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.LayoutElement>();
                layoutElement.flexibleWidth = 1;
                
                // Force layout rebuild to get accurate width, then calculate height
                UnityEngine.UI.LayoutRebuilder.ForceRebuildLayoutImmediate(rectTransform);
                textComponent.ForceMeshUpdate();
                
                // Use GetPreferredValues with a specific width to get wrapped height
                UnityEngine.Vector2 preferredSize = textComponent.GetPreferredValues(sb.ToString(), textWidth, 0);
                float preferredHeight = preferredSize.y;
                
                // Set the preferred height on the layout element
                layoutElement.preferredHeight = preferredHeight + 20f; // Add padding
                layoutElement.minHeight = preferredHeight + 20f;
                
                // Also set the rect height explicitly
                rectTransform.SetSizeWithCurrentAnchors(UnityEngine.RectTransform.Axis.Vertical, preferredHeight + 20f);
```

```
        public static void ChangeDisplay_Postfix(Assets.Scripts.UI.UniversalPage __instance, StationpediaPage page)
        {
            try
            {
                // Unity-safe null checks - destroyed objects are "fake null"
                if ((object)__instance == null || !__instance) return;
                if ((object)page == null) return;
                
                // Check if the Content transform is valid (not destroyed)
                UnityEngine.Transform contentTransform;
                try
                {
                    contentTransform = __instance.Content;
                    if ((object)contentTransform == null || !contentTransform) return;
                    // Try to access gameObject to verify it's not destroyed
                    var testGO = contentTransform.gameObject;
                    if ((object)testGO == null || !testGO) return;
                }
                catch
                {
                    return; // Content is destroyed
                }
                
                string pageKey = page.Key;
                if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(pageKey)) return;

                if (!StationpediaPlusMod.DeviceDatabase.TryGetValue(pageKey, out var deviceDesc)) return;
                if (deviceDesc.specialBehaviors == null || deviceDesc.specialBehaviors.Count == 0) return;

                // Check if we already added Special Behaviors (prevent duplicates on hot-reload)
                foreach (var existingCat in __instance.CreatedCategories)
                {
                    if (existingCat != null && existingCat.Title != null && 
                        existingCat.Title.text == "Special Behaviors")
                    {
                        return; // Already exists
                    }
                }

                var stationpedia = Stationpedia.Instance;
                if ((object)stationpedia == null || !stationpedia) return;
                
                var categoryPrefab = stationpedia.CategoryPrefab;
                var genericPrefab = stationpedia.GenericPrefab;
                if ((object)categoryPrefab == null || !categoryPrefab) return;
                if ((object)genericPrefab == null || !genericPrefab) return;

                // Create the collapsible category container (like "Logic" or "Internal Memory")
                StationpediaCategory category = UnityEngine.Object.Instantiate<StationpediaCategory>(
                    categoryPrefab, contentTransform);
                if ((object)category == null || !category) return;
                if ((object)category.Title == null || !category.Title) return;
                if ((object)category.Contents == null || !category.Contents) return;
                
                category.Title.text = "Special Behaviors";

                // Create ONE SPDAGeneric item for ALL behaviors combined
                // This follows the same pattern as PopulateLogicInstructions
                SPDAGeneric item = UnityEngine.Object.Instantiate<SPDAGeneric>(
                    genericPrefab, category.Contents);
                
                if ((object)item == null || !item) return;

                // Build combined text for all behaviors
                var sb = new System.Text.StringBuilder();
                bool first = true;
                foreach (var behavior in deviceDesc.specialBehaviors)
                {
                    if (!first) sb.AppendLine().AppendLine(); // Double line break between behaviors
                    sb.AppendLine($"<color=#FFD700><b>{behavior.title}</b></color>");
                    sb.Append(behavior.description);
                    first = false;
                }
                
                // Use the Header for the text (it's the larger text field in SPDAGeneric)
                if ((object)item.Header != null && item.Header)
                {
                    item.Header.text = sb.ToString();
                    item.Header.enableWordWrapping = true;
                    item.Header.overflowMode = TMPro.TextOverflowModes.Overflow;
                    item.Header.richText = true;
                }
                
                // Hide the Info text and Icon
                if ((object)item.Info != null && item.Info)
                {
                    item.Info.gameObject.SetActive(false);
                }
                if ((object)item.Icon != null && item.Icon)
                {
                    item.Icon.gameObject.SetActive(false);
                }

                // Make sure contents are visible
                category.Contents.gameObject.SetActive(true);
                
                // Add to game's category list so it handles cleanup
                __instance.CreatedCategories.Add(category);
            }
            catch (Exception)
            {
            }
        }
```

- oldString:

```
        public static void ChangeDisplay_Postfix(Assets.Scripts.UI.UniversalPage __instance, StationpediaPage page)
        {
            try
            {
                // Unity-safe null checks - destroyed objects are "fake null"
                if ((object)__instance == null || !__instance) return;
                if ((object)page == null) return;
                
                // Check if the Content transform is valid (not destroyed)
                UnityEngine.Transform contentTransform;
                try
                {
                    contentTransform = __instance.Content;
                    if ((object)contentTransform == null || !contentTransform) return;
                    // Try to access gameObject to verify it's not destroyed
                    var testGO = contentTransform.gameObject;
                    if ((object)testGO == null || !testGO) return;
                }
                catch
                {
                    return; // Content is destroyed
                }
                
                string pageKey = page.Key;
                if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(pageKey)) return;

                if (!StationpediaPlusMod.DeviceDatabase.TryGetValue(pageKey, out var deviceDesc)) return;
                if (deviceDesc.specialBehaviors == null || deviceDesc.specialBehaviors.Count == 0) return;

                var stationpedia = Stationpedia.Instance;
                if ((object)stationpedia == null || !stationpedia) return;
                
                var categoryPrefab = stationpedia.CategoryPrefab;
                if ((object)categoryPrefab == null || !categoryPrefab) return;

                // Create the collapsible category container (like "Logic" or "Internal Memory")
                StationpediaCategory category = UnityEngine.Object.Instantiate<StationpediaCategory>(
                    categoryPrefab, contentTransform);
                if ((object)category == null || !category) return;
                if ((object)category.Title == null || !category.Title) return;
                if ((object)category.Contents == null || !category.Contents) return;
                
                category.Title.text = "Special Behaviors";

                // Build combined text for all behaviors
                var sb = new System.Text.StringBuilder();
                bool first = true;
                foreach (var behavior in deviceDesc.specialBehaviors)
                {
                    if (!first) sb.AppendLine().AppendLine(); // Double line break between behaviors
                    sb.AppendLine($"<color=#FFD700><b>{behavior.title}</b></color>");
                    sb.Append(behavior.description);
                    first = false;
                }
                
                // Instead of using SPDAGeneric prefab (which has fixed height), create a raw TMP text element
                // This lets the layout system size it properly based on content
                var textGO = new UnityEngine.GameObject("SpecialBehaviorsText");
                textGO.transform.SetParent(category.Contents, false);
                
                // Add RectTransform (required for UI)
                var rectTransform = textGO.AddComponent<UnityEngine.RectTransform>();
                
                // Get parent width to calculate wrapped text height correctly
                float parentWidth = category.Contents.rect.width;
                if (parentWidth <= 0) parentWidth = 400f; // Fallback width
                
                // Set size explicitly - stretch to parent width
                rectTransform.anchorMin = new UnityEngine.Vector2(0, 1);
                rectTransform.anchorMax = new UnityEngine.Vector2(1, 1);
                rectTransform.pivot = new UnityEngine.Vector2(0.5f, 1);
                rectTransform.sizeDelta = new UnityEngine.Vector2(0, 100); // Width from anchors, temp height
                
                // Add the text component
                var textComponent = textGO.AddComponent<TMPro.TextMeshProUGUI>();
                textComponent.text = sb.ToString();
                textComponent.enableWordWrapping = true;
                textComponent.overflowMode = TMPro.TextOverflowModes.Overflow;
                textComponent.richText = true;
                textComponent.fontSize = 16;
                textComponent.color = UnityEngine.Color.white;
                textComponent.margin = new UnityEngine.Vector4(10, 5, 10, 5); // left, top, right, bottom padding
                
                // Set the rect width explicitly so TMP can calculate wrapped height
                float textWidth = parentWidth - 20f; // Account for margins
                textComponent.rectTransform.SetSizeWithCurrentAnchors(UnityEngine.RectTransform.Axis.Horizontal, textWidth);
                
                // Add LayoutElement so the vertical layout group respects preferred size
                var layoutElement = textGO.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.LayoutElement>();
                layoutElement.flexibleWidth = 1;
                
                // Force layout rebuild to get accurate width, then calculate height
                UnityEngine.UI.LayoutRebuilder.ForceRebuildLayoutImmediate(rectTransform);
                textComponent.ForceMeshUpdate();
                
                // Use GetPreferredValues with a specific width to get wrapped height
                UnityEngine.Vector2 preferredSize = textComponent.GetPreferredValues(sb.ToString(), textWidth, 0);
                float preferredHeight = preferredSize.y;
                
                // Set the preferred height on the layout element
                layoutElement.preferredHeight = preferredHeight + 20f; // Add padding
                layoutElement.minHeight = preferredHeight + 20f;
                
                // Also set the rect height explicitly
                rectTransform.SetSizeWithCurrentAnchors(UnityEngine.RectTransform.Axis.Vertical, preferredHeight + 20f);
                
                // Make sure contents are visible
                category.Contents.gameObject.SetActive(true);
                
                // Add to game's category list so it handles cleanup
                __instance.CreatedCategories.Add(category);
            }
            catch (Exception)
            {
            }
        }
```


</details></tool-use>

```
        public static void ChangeDisplay_Postfix(Assets.Scripts.UI.UniversalPage __instance, StationpediaPage page)
        {
            try
            {
                // Unity-safe null checks - destroyed objects are "fake null"
                if ((object)__instance == null || !__instance) return;
                if ((object)page == null) return;
                
                // Check if the Content transform is valid (not destroyed)
                UnityEngine.Transform contentTransform;
                try
                {
                    contentTransform = __instance.Content;
                    if ((object)contentTransform == null || !contentTransform) return;
                    var testGO = contentTransform.gameObject;
                    if ((object)testGO == null || !testGO) return;
                }
                catch
                {
                    return;
                }
                
                string pageKey = page.Key;
                if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(pageKey)) return;

                if (!StationpediaPlusMod.DeviceDatabase.TryGetValue(pageKey, out var deviceDesc)) return;
                if (deviceDesc.specialBehaviors == null || deviceDesc.specialBehaviors.Count == 0) return;

                // Check for existing by GameObject name (more reliable than checking CreatedCategories)
                var existingCategory = contentTransform.Find("SpecialBehaviorsCategory");
                if (existingCategory != null)
                {
                    return; // Already exists
                }

                var stationpedia = Stationpedia.Instance;
                if ((object)stationpedia == null || !stationpedia) return;
                
                var categoryPrefab = stationpedia.CategoryPrefab;
                if ((object)categoryPrefab == null || !categoryPrefab) return;

                if ((object)sourceText == null || !sourceText) return;

                // Create the collapsible category container
                StationpediaCategory category = UnityEngine.Object.Instantiate<StationpediaCategory>(
                    categoryPrefab, contentTransform);
                if ((object)category == null || !category) return;
                
                // Name it so we can find it later to prevent duplicates
                category.gameObject.name = "SpecialBehaviorsCategory";
                
                if ((object)category.Title == null || !category.Title) return;
                if ((object)category.Contents == null || !category.Contents) return;
                
                category.Title.text = "Special Behaviors";

                // Build combined text for all behaviors

                bool first = true;

                {
                    if (!first) sb.AppendLine().AppendLine();
                    sb.AppendLine($"<color=#FFD700><b>{behavior.title}</b></color>");
                    sb.Append(behavior.description);
                    first = false;

                textComponent.richText = true;
                textComponent.text = sb.ToString();
                textComponent.margin = new UnityEngine.Vector4(10, 5, 10, 10);
                
                // Add ContentSizeFitter to auto-size height based on text
                var fitter = textGO.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter>();
                fitter.horizontalFit = UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.Unconstrained;
                fitter.verticalFit = UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.PreferredSize;
                
                // Add LayoutElement for the layout group
```

```
        public static void ChangeDisplay_Postfix(Assets.Scripts.UI.UniversalPage __instance, StationpediaPage page)
        {
            try
            {
                // Unity-safe null checks - destroyed objects are "fake null"
                if ((object)__instance == null || !__instance) return;
                if ((object)page == null) return;
                
                // Check if the Content transform is valid (not destroyed)
                UnityEngine.Transform contentTransform;
                try
                {
                    contentTransform = __instance.Content;
                    if ((object)contentTransform == null || !contentTransform) return;
                    // Try to access gameObject to verify it's not destroyed
                    var testGO = contentTransform.gameObject;
                    if ((object)testGO == null || !testGO) return;
                }
                catch
                {
                    return; // Content is destroyed
                }
                
                string pageKey = page.Key;
                if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(pageKey)) return;

                if (!StationpediaPlusMod.DeviceDatabase.TryGetValue(pageKey, out var deviceDesc)) return;
                if (deviceDesc.specialBehaviors == null || deviceDesc.specialBehaviors.Count == 0) return;

                // Check if we already added Special Behaviors (prevent duplicates on hot-reload)
                foreach (var existingCat in __instance.CreatedCategories)
                {
                    if (existingCat != null && existingCat.Title != null && 
                        existingCat.Title.text == "Special Behaviors")
                    {
                        return; // Already exists
                    }
                }

                var stationpedia = Stationpedia.Instance;
                if ((object)stationpedia == null || !stationpedia) return;
                
                var categoryPrefab = stationpedia.CategoryPrefab;
                var genericPrefab = stationpedia.GenericPrefab;
                if ((object)categoryPrefab == null || !categoryPrefab) return;
                if ((object)genericPrefab == null || !genericPrefab) return;

                // Create the collapsible category container (like "Logic" or "Internal Memory")
                StationpediaCategory category = UnityEngine.Object.Instantiate<StationpediaCategory>(
                    categoryPrefab, contentTransform);
                if ((object)category == null || !category) return;
                if ((object)category.Title == null || !category.Title) return;
                if ((object)category.Contents == null || !category.Contents) return;
                
                category.Title.text = "Special Behaviors";

                // Create ONE SPDAGeneric item for ALL behaviors combined
                // This follows the same pattern as PopulateLogicInstructions
                SPDAGeneric item = UnityEngine.Object.Instantiate<SPDAGeneric>(
                    genericPrefab, category.Contents);
                
                if ((object)item == null || !item) return;

                // Build combined text for all behaviors
                var sb = new System.Text.StringBuilder();
                bool first = true;
                foreach (var behavior in deviceDesc.specialBehaviors)
                {
                    if (!first) sb.AppendLine().AppendLine(); // Double line break between behaviors
                    sb.AppendLine($"<color=#FFD700><b>{behavior.title}</b></color>");
                    sb.Append(behavior.description);
                    first = false;
                }
                
                // Use the Header for the text (it's the larger text field in SPDAGeneric)
                if ((object)item.Header != null && item.Header)
                {
                    item.Header.text = sb.ToString();
                    item.Header.enableWordWrapping = true;
                    item.Header.overflowMode = TMPro.TextOverflowModes.Overflow;
                    item.Header.richText = true;
                }
                
                // Hide the Info text and Icon
                if ((object)item.Info != null && item.Info)
                {
                    item.Info.gameObject.SetActive(false);
                }
                if ((object)item.Icon != null && item.Icon)
                {
                    item.Icon.gameObject.SetActive(false);
                }

                // Make sure contents are visible
                category.Contents.gameObject.SetActive(true);
                
                // Add to game's category list so it handles cleanup
                __instance.CreatedCategories.Add(category);
            }
            catch (Exception)
```

```
                // Create a text GameObject inside the category contents
                var textGO = new UnityEngine.GameObject("BehaviorsText");
                textGO.transform.SetParent(category.Contents, false);
                
                // Add TextMeshProUGUI and copy settings from PageDescription
                var textComponent = textGO.AddComponent<TMPro.TextMeshProUGUI>();
                textComponent.font = sourceText.font;
                textComponent.fontSharedMaterial = sourceText.fontSharedMaterial;
                textComponent.fontSize = sourceText.fontSize;
                textComponent.color = sourceText.color;
                textComponent.enableWordWrapping = true;
                textComponent.overflowMode = TMPro.TextOverflowModes.Overflow;
                textComponent.richText = true;
                textComponent.text = sb.ToString();
                
                // Set up RectTransform for proper anchoring
                var rectTransform = textGO.GetComponent<UnityEngine.RectTransform>();
                rectTransform.anchorMin = new UnityEngine.Vector2(0, 1);
                rectTransform.anchorMax = new UnityEngine.Vector2(1, 1);
                rectTransform.pivot = new UnityEngine.Vector2(0.5f, 1);
                rectTransform.sizeDelta = new UnityEngine.Vector2(-20, 0); // Horizontal padding
                
                // Add LayoutElement for the layout group
                var layoutElement = textGO.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.LayoutElement>();
                layoutElement.flexibleWidth = 1;
                layoutElement.preferredWidth = -1;
                
                // Ensure category.Contents has a VerticalLayoutGroup for proper sizing
                var contentsLayout = category.Contents.GetComponent<UnityEngine.UI.VerticalLayoutGroup>();
                if (contentsLayout == null)
                {
                    contentsLayout = category.Contents.gameObject.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.VerticalLayoutGroup>();
                    contentsLayout.childForceExpandWidth = true;
                    contentsLayout.childForceExpandHeight = false;
                    contentsLayout.childControlWidth = true;
                    contentsLayout.childControlHeight = true;
                    contentsLayout.spacing = 5;
                    contentsLayout.padding = new UnityEngine.RectOffset(10, 10, 5, 10);
                }
                
                // Ensure category.Contents has a ContentSizeFitter to expand based on children
                var contentsFitter = category.Contents.GetComponent<UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter>();
                if (contentsFitter == null)
```

```
                string pageKey = page.Key;
                if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(pageKey)) return;

                // DEBUG: Log what we're looking for
                UnityEngine.Debug.Log($"[StationpediaPlus] ChangeDisplay for pageKey: {pageKey}");

                if (!StationpediaPlusMod.DeviceDatabase.TryGetValue(pageKey, out var deviceDesc)) return;
                if (deviceDesc.specialBehaviors == null || deviceDesc.specialBehaviors.Count == 0) return;
                
                // DEBUG: Log how many behaviors we found
                UnityEngine.Debug.Log($"[StationpediaPlus] Found {deviceDesc.specialBehaviors.Count} special behaviors for {pageKey}");
```

- newString:

```
                // Add LayoutElement with explicit preferred height
                var layoutElement = textGO.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.LayoutElement>();
                layoutElement.flexibleWidth = 1;
                layoutElement.preferredHeight = testHeight;
                layoutElement.minHeight = testHeight;
                
                // The Contents has a GridLayoutGroup by default which forces fixed cell sizes
                // We need to disable it and use a VerticalLayoutGroup instead
                var existingGridLayout = category.Contents.GetComponent<UnityEngine.UI.GridLayoutGroup>();
                if (existingGridLayout != null)
                {
                    existingGridLayout.enabled = false;
                    UnityEngine.Debug.Log($"[StationpediaPlus] Disabled existing GridLayoutGroup");
                }
                
                // Now add our VerticalLayoutGroup
                var contentsLayout = category.Contents.GetComponent<UnityEngine.UI.VerticalLayoutGroup>();
                if (contentsLayout == null)
                {
                    contentsLayout = category.Contents.gameObject.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.VerticalLayoutGroup>();
                    contentsLayout.childForceExpandWidth = true;
                    contentsLayout.childForceExpandHeight = false;
                    contentsLayout.childControlWidth = true;
                    contentsLayout.childControlHeight = true;
                    contentsLayout.spacing = 5;
                    contentsLayout.padding = new UnityEngine.RectOffset(10, 10, 5, 10);
                }
                
                // Ensure category.Contents has a ContentSizeFitter to expand based on children
                var contentsFitter = category.Contents.GetComponent<UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter>();
                if (contentsFitter == null)
                {
                    contentsFitter = category.Contents.gameObject.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter>();
                    contentsFitter.verticalFit = UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.PreferredSize;
                    contentsFitter.horizontalFit = UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.Unconstrained;
                }
                
                // Make sure contents are visible
                category.Contents.gameObject.SetActive(true);
                
                // Force layout rebuild to calculate correct sizes
                UnityEngine.UI.LayoutRebuilder.ForceRebuildLayoutImmediate(category.Contents);
                UnityEngine.UI.LayoutRebuilder.ForceRebuildLayoutImmediate(contentTransform as UnityEngine.RectTransform);
                
                // Add to game's category list so it handles cleanup
                __instance.CreatedCategories.Add(category);
```

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

Now I see the problem! The log shows:

1. Full text length: 1818 chars - all 7 behaviors ARE in the text
2. Text preview shows multiple behaviors with proper formatting
3. The ERROR: "Can't add 'VerticalLayoutGroup' to RuntimeContents because a 'GridLayoutGroup' is already added to the game object!"

So `category.Contents` already has a `GridLayoutGroup` on it! When I try to add a `VerticalLayoutGroup`, it fails because Unity only allows one LayoutGroup per GameObject.

The GridLayoutGroup is the problem - it's arranging items in a grid, which is why the text is being clipped. We need to NOT add our own VerticalLayoutGroup (since one already exists), and instead work with what's there or put our text in a different container structure.

Let me fix this by:
1. NOT adding VerticalLayoutGroup or ContentSizeFitter to category.Contents
2. Instead, just make sure our text element has proper sizing

The issue is the GridLayoutGroup is forcing a fixed cell size. We need to either:
- Remove/disable the GridLayoutGroup
- Or create a different structure

Let me try disabling the existing GridLayoutGroup and adding our own VerticalLayoutGroup instead.

**Input:**

- filePath: `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\mod\StationpediaPlus.cs`
- newString:

```
                layoutElement.preferredHeight = testHeight;
                layoutElement.minHeight = testHeight;
                
                // The Contents has a GridLayoutGroup by default which forces fixed cell sizes
                // We need to DESTROY it (not just disable) so we can add a VerticalLayoutGroup
                var existingGridLayout = category.Contents.GetComponent<UnityEngine.UI.GridLayoutGroup>();
                if (existingGridLayout != null)
                {
                    UnityEngine.Object.DestroyImmediate(existingGridLayout);
                    UnityEngine.Debug.Log($"[StationpediaPlus] Destroyed existing GridLayoutGroup");
                }
                
                // Now add our VerticalLayoutGroup
                var contentsLayout = category.Contents.GetComponent<UnityEngine.UI.VerticalLayoutGroup>();
                if (contentsLayout == null)
                {
                    contentsLayout = category.Contents.gameObject.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.VerticalLayoutGroup>();
                    contentsLayout.childForceExpandWidth = true;
                    contentsLayout.childForceExpandHeight = false;
                    contentsLayout.childControlWidth = true;
                    contentsLayout.childControlHeight = true;
                    contentsLayout.spacing = 5;
                    contentsLayout.padding = new UnityEngine.RectOffset(10, 10, 5, 10);
                }
                
                // Ensure category.Contents has a ContentSizeFitter to expand based on children
                var contentsFitter = category.Contents.GetComponent<UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter>();
                if (contentsFitter == null)
                {
                    contentsFitter = category.Contents.gameObject.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter>();
                    contentsFitter.verticalFit = UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.PreferredSize;
```

- oldString:

```
                layoutElement.preferredHeight = testHeight;
                layoutElement.minHeight = testHeight;
                
                // The Contents has a GridLayoutGroup by default which forces fixed cell sizes
                // We need to disable it and use a VerticalLayoutGroup instead
                var existingGridLayout = category.Contents.GetComponent<UnityEngine.UI.GridLayoutGroup>();
                if (existingGridLayout != null)
                {
                    existingGridLayout.enabled = false;
                    UnityEngine.Debug.Log($"[StationpediaPlus] Disabled existing GridLayoutGroup");
                }
                
                // Now add our VerticalLayoutGroup
                var contentsLayout = category.Contents.GetComponent<UnityEngine.UI.VerticalLayoutGroup>();
                if (contentsLayout == null)
                {
                    contentsLayout = category.Contents.gameObject.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.VerticalLayoutGroup>();
                    contentsLayout.childForceExpandWidth = true;
                    contentsLayout.childForceExpandHeight = false;
                    contentsLayout.childControlWidth = true;
                    contentsLayout.childControlHeight = true;
                    contentsLayout.spacing = 5;
                    contentsLayout.padding = new UnityEngine.RectOffset(10, 10, 5, 10);
                }
                
                // Ensure category.Contents has a ContentSizeFitter to expand based on children
                var contentsFitter = category.Contents.GetComponent<UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter>();
                if (contentsFitter == null)
                {
                    contentsFitter = category.Contents.gameObject.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter>();
                    contentsFitter.verticalFit = UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.PreferredSize;
```

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

The user is saying:
1. "Category already exists, skipping creation" keeps showing - the old broken category is persisting
2. On device pages it only shows 1 behavior
3. On kit pages it was showing everywhere (wrongly)

The problem is that the category exists from the first broken creation and never gets properly recreated. The user needs to navigate away from Aimee and come back to a FRESH page, or we need to destroy the old category when we detect it was created with the broken layout.

Actually, looking more carefully - the issue is the old category exists with only 1 behavior showing because the GridLayoutGroup was clipping it. And since it exists, we skip creating a new one.

Let me take a different approach - instead of trying to fix the layout issues, let me:
1. NOT use the category.Contents at all
2. Create a completely new container with the right layout
3. Or just skip all the layout group stuff and just set the height directly on the RectTransform

Actually, the simplest fix would be to:
1. When we find an existing category, check if it has proper layout, and if not, destroy it and recreate
2. Or simpler - just delete the existing one and always recreate

Let me change the logic to destroy the existing category if found and recreate it fresh. That way the fix will apply.

But wait - looking at the code, the text GameObject is being created and added to `category.Contents`. The VerticalLayoutGroup with the padding is also on `category.Contents`. The padding should affect the children.

But there's another issue - we're setting up the RectTransform anchors before setting up the layout group. Let me look more carefully at what's happening.

Actually, the issue might be that the text is being positioned by its RectTransform anchors rather than the layout group. We're setting:
```csharp
rectTransform.anchorMin = new UnityEngine.Vector2(0, 1);
rectTransform.anchorMax = new UnityEngine.Vector2(1, 1);
rectTransform.pivot = new UnityEngine.Vector2(0.5f, 1);
```

This anchors the text to the top. The layout group padding should still work if the layout is controlling the children... but maybe there's a conflict.

Let me also check if the text's margin could help. Actually, looking at the code flow:
1. textGO is created
2. textComponent (TMP) is added
3. RectTransform is configured
4. LayoutElement is added
5. GridLayoutGroup is destroyed
6. Image is set up

```
                // Patch ChangeDisplay to add Special Behaviors section
                var changeDisplay = universalPageType.GetMethod("ChangeDisplay", 
                    BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.Instance);
                if (changeDisplay != null)
                {
                    var postfix = typeof(HarmonyPatches).GetMethod("ChangeDisplay_Postfix", 
                        BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.Static);
                    _harmony.Patch(changeDisplay, postfix: new HarmonyMethod(postfix));
                    ConsoleWindow.Print("[StationpediaPlus] Patched ChangeDisplay");
                }
                else
                {
                    ConsoleWindow.Print("[StationpediaPlus] ERROR: Could not find ChangeDisplay method");
                }
                
                // Patch DraggingEnabled to allow dragging in main menu
                var stationpediaType = typeof(Stationpedia);
                var draggingEnabled = stationpediaType.GetProperty("DraggingEnabled", 
                    BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.Instance);
                if (draggingEnabled != null)
                {
                    var getter = draggingEnabled.GetGetMethod();
                    var prefix = typeof(HarmonyPatches).GetMethod("DraggingEnabled_Prefix", 
                        BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.Static);
                    _harmony.Patch(getter, prefix: new HarmonyMethod(prefix));
                    ConsoleWindow.Print("[StationpediaPlus] Patched DraggingEnabled - window now draggable in main menu");
                }
                else
                {
                    ConsoleWindow.Print("[StationpediaPlus] Could not find DraggingEnabled property");
                }
                
                ConsoleWindow.Print("[StationpediaPlus] Harmony patches applied successfully");
```

```
        // Prefix to enable dragging in main menu
        public static bool DraggingEnabled_Prefix(ref bool __result)
        {
            __result = true; // Always allow dragging
            return false; // Skip original method
        }
        
        // Postfix to clamp window to screen after dragging (works in main menu too)
        public static void OnDrag_Postfix(Assets.Scripts.UI.DraggableWindow __instance)
        {
            try
            {
                var rectTransform = __instance.RectTransform;
                if (rectTransform == null) return;
                
                // Get screen bounds
                float screenWidth = UnityEngine.Screen.width;
                float screenHeight = UnityEngine.Screen.height;
                
                // Get window position
                Vector3 pos = rectTransform.position;
                
                // Clamp to screen with some margin so window is always grabbable
                float margin = 100f;
                pos.x = Mathf.Clamp(pos.x, margin, screenWidth - margin);
                pos.y = Mathf.Clamp(pos.y, margin, screenHeight - margin);
                
                rectTransform.position = pos;
            }
            catch { }
        }
        
        private static bool IsSlotNumberList(string text)
```

```
        private void RegisterConsoleCommands()
        {
            try
            {
                // Check if command already exists (for F6 reload)
                if (Util.Commands.CommandLine.CommandsMap.ContainsKey("stationpediacenter"))
                {
                    return; // Already registered
                }
                
                // Register "stationpediacenter" command to center the Stationpedia window
                Util.Commands.CommandLine.AddCommand("stationpediacenter", 
                    new Util.Commands.BasicCommand(CenterStationpediaCommand, 
                        "Centers the Stationpedia window on screen", null, false));
                ConsoleWindow.Print("[StationpediaPlus] Registered console command: stationpediacenter");
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                ConsoleWindow.Print($"[StationpediaPlus] Error registering console commands: {ex.Message}");
            }
        }
```

```
        // Store drag offset for our custom OnDrag
        private static Vector3 _dragOffset;
        
        // Prefix to replace OnDrag entirely - the original calls ClampToScreen which crashes in main menu
        public static bool Stationpedia_OnDrag_Prefix(Stationpedia __instance, UnityEngine.EventSystems.PointerEventData eventData)
        {
            try
            {
                // Simple drag implementation that doesn't call ClampToScreen
                Vector3 mousePos = UnityEngine.Input.mousePosition;
                __instance.RectTransform.position = mousePos - _dragOffset;
            }
            catch { }
            return false; // Skip original
        }
        
        // We also need to patch OnBeginDrag to capture the offset
        public static bool Stationpedia_OnBeginDrag_Prefix(Stationpedia __instance, UnityEngine.EventSystems.PointerEventData eventData)
        {
            try
            {
                _dragOffset = UnityEngine.Input.mousePosition - __instance.RectTransform.position;
            }
            catch { }
            return false; // Skip original
        }
        
        private static bool IsSlotNumberList(string text)
```

```
                // Calculate height based on text content
                // Account for word wrapping by estimating ~45 chars per line at this width
                string textContent = sb.ToString();
                int explicitLineCount = textContent.Split('\n').Length;
                int estimatedWrappedLines = textContent.Length / 40; // Rough estimate for word wrap (narrower due to margins)
                int totalLines = System.Math.Max(explicitLineCount, estimatedWrappedLines);
                float estimatedHeight = (totalLines * 18f) + 40f; // 18px per line + 40px padding for margins
```

What is 40px a measurement of

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

In the context of Unity UI, `40px` (40 pixels) is a measurement of screen/UI space. In our height calculation:

```csharp
float estimatedHeight = (totalLines * 18f) + 40f;
```

- **18f per line** - Estimated height for each line of text (font size + line spacing)
- **40f padding** - Extra space added to account for:
  - The TMP margin we set (10px top + 10px bottom = 20px)
  - The VerticalLayoutGroup padding (10px top + 10px bottom = 20px)
  - Any rounding errors in the line estimate

It's basically a safety buffer so the background doesn't cut off the last line. The `f` suffix just means it's a `float` value in C#.

If it's still cutting off, we can increase it further - the number is just a guess since we can't precisely measure how TMP will render the text until runtime.

---
